skill.name: devops-runbooks
skill.description: Creates structured DevOps runbooks for incident response, maintenance procedures, rollback plans, troubleshooting, and escalation paths.
skill.summary: Create practical operational runbooks for incidents, maintenance, rollback, and on-call response.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown templates and placeholder tokens. The skill does not execute commands, access secrets, or contain prompt-injection instructions; the remaining risk is that users may place operational commands into generated documentation.

low_findings.title: Static Command Detections Are Documentation Placeholders
low_findings.description: The detected shell-command locations are Markdown code fences with template placeholders for runbook authors. No evidence found that the skill executes these commands or invokes a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and template syntax, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill has no script file or runtime command invocation in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Credential References Are Location Placeholders
low_findings.description: The sensitive-data findings point to placeholders for system names and credential locations. They do not contain credentials, secret values, or instructions to expose secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The template asks authors to identify access requirements and vault locations, which is normal for runbooks. No concrete secret material appears at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Signals Are Placeholder Text
low_findings.description: The prompt-injection detections appear to come from placeholder names containing the word system. No evidence found of override language or instructions to bypass analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited placeholders are variables such as system names, not messages claiming authority over the model. There is no skip-review, override, or role-changing instruction nearby.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain runbook step descriptions, issue text, or skill prose. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing functions, or security-sensitive crypto usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found generic documentation text and placeholders only. Confidence is high, though the static pattern source is not visible in the report.

content.value_statement: Incident teams often lack consistent operational procedures. This skill structures runbooks with prerequisites, commands, rollback steps, troubleshooting, and escalation paths.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured runbooks with overview, prerequisites, procedure, verification, rollback, troubleshooting, and escalation sections.
Documents incident response steps for on-call teams and service owners.
Adds quick-reference tables for commands, dashboards, logs, metrics, and contacts.
Defines verification criteria and expected outcomes for operational procedures.
Captures rollback steps and escalation paths for failed procedures.
limitations: Does not verify that commands are safe for a specific production environment.
Does not execute procedures or inspect live infrastructure by itself.
Requires service owners to provide accurate systems, contacts, URLs, and access details.
Generated runbooks should be reviewed and tested before operational use.

use_cases.title: Prepare On-Call Incident Procedures
use_cases.description: Create a clear response guide with triggers, triage steps, verification checks, rollback instructions, and escalation contacts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Maintenance Workflows
use_cases.description: Turn recurring maintenance work into a repeatable runbook that includes prerequisites, commands, expected output, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Capture Service Knowledge
use_cases.description: Document service-specific troubleshooting knowledge so new team members can follow approved operational procedures.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Runbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new operational procedure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a runbook for restarting SERVICE_NAME. Include prerequisites, exact steps, verification, rollback, troubleshooting, and escalation contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Document an Incident Response
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for on-call incident handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an incident response runbook for high error rates in SERVICE_NAME. Include triggers, dashboards, log checks, decision points, and escalation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes Into a Runbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using rough operational notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these maintenance notes into a structured runbook. Add missing prerequisites, verification criteria, expected outputs, rollback steps, and troubleshooting guidance: NOTES.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Harden a Runbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for operational readiness review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this runbook for production readiness. Identify unclear steps, unsafe assumptions, missing rollback paths, weak verification, and gaps in escalation: RUNBOOK_TEXT.

output_examples.input: Request to create a database failover runbook.
output_examples.output: A complete runbook with purpose, triggers, access requirements, failover procedure, health checks, rollback plan, troubleshooting cases, and escalation path.

output_examples.input: Rough notes for rotating service credentials.
output_examples.output: A structured maintenance procedure with prerequisites, owner approvals, step-by-step rotation tasks, validation checks, rollback guidance, and post-change review items.

output_examples.input: Existing runbook that lacks verification and rollback details.
output_examples.output: A review summary that highlights missing safety checks, adds concrete success criteria, and recommends rollback and escalation improvements.

best_practices: Provide real service names, ownership details, severity levels, and operational context before generating a runbook.
Ask for expected output, success criteria, rollback steps, and escalation contacts for every procedure.
Review generated commands with service owners before adding the runbook to production documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated runbooks without testing the procedure in a safe environment.
Do not include raw passwords, tokens, private keys, or secret values in runbook text.
Do not leave placeholders unresolved before using a runbook during an incident.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates operational runbooks for incidents, maintenance, troubleshooting, rollback, and escalation workflows.

faq.question: Can it execute the commands in a runbook?
faq.answer: No. It helps document commands and procedures, but it does not execute operational commands.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production operations?
faq.answer: Yes, but generated runbooks should be reviewed, tested, and approved before production use.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing runbook?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify missing prerequisites, unclear steps, weak verification, rollback gaps, and escalation issues.

faq.question: Should secrets be included in the runbook?
faq.answer: No. Reference approved vault locations or access processes instead of writing secret values.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
